evals.name: NLFM Design via Stationary Phase
evals.description: Tests whether the agent uses nlfmspec2freq with CustomFMWaveform instead of building NLFM from scratch
evals.prompt: Design an NLFM waveform with -40 dB peak sidelobes using the stationary phase principle. Use 100 microsecond pulse width, 5 MHz bandwidth. Show me the code to create the waveform and measure its sidelobe level after matched filtering. Save the complete script as a .m file.

context.setup: Requires Phased Array System Toolbox and Signal Processing Toolbox

assertions.description: Uses nlfmspec2freq to compute frequency profile
Uses phased.CustomFMWaveform (not NonlinearFMWaveform)
Does not use nlfmspec2freq with NonlinearFMWaveform
Uses sidelobelevel function for measurement
Passes dB values to sidelobelevel (mag2db or 20*log10)
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Periodic Ambiguity Function for FMCW
evals.description: Tests whether agent uses pambgfun (not ambgfun) for CW waveform Doppler analysis and does not try getMatchedFilter
evals.prompt: I have an FMCW waveform with 150 MHz bandwidth and 7 microsecond sweep time for automotive radar. Compute its ambiguity function to analyze Doppler resolution. Use 10 periods for the analysis. Save the complete script as a .m file.

assertions.description: Uses pambgfun for periodic ambiguity function
Uses phased.FMCWWaveform (not LinearFMWaveform or manual signal)
assertions.expected: The generated code creates the FMCW waveform using phased.FMCWWaveform, not phased.LinearFMWaveform or manual signal construction

assertions.description: Does not call getMatchedFilter on FMCW object (method does not exist)
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Parameter Derivation from Requirements
evals.description: Tests whether the agent derives waveform parameters from system requirements using toolbox functions, uses 2*speed2dop for two-way Doppler, and sets sufficient sample rate
evals.prompt: I'm designing a pulsed radar at 10 GHz. Requirements: range resolution 15 m, max unambiguous range 50 km, max target velocity 200 m/s. Derive the waveform parameters (bandwidth, PRF, max Doppler, pulse width with TBP=100) and create an LFM waveform. Measure its sidelobe level after matched filtering. Save the complete script as a .m file.

assertions.description: Uses rangeres2bw to derive bandwidth from range resolution
Uses 2*speed2dop for two-way radar Doppler (not speed2dop alone)
Sets SampleRate >= 8*bandwidth for accurate PSL measurement
Uses sidelobelevel with dB input
Does not hardcode bandwidth (should derive from rangeres2bw)
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Deep Sidelobe Phase-Coded Waveform
evals.description: Tests whether the agent recommends pnkcode for deep PSL targets rather than Legendre or binary codes, and handles SampleRate*ChipWidth constraint
evals.prompt: I need a phase-coded waveform with peak sidelobes below -30 dB and about 400 chips. The chip width should give approximately 5 MHz bandwidth. Sample rate is 50 MHz. Show me the code to create the waveform and measure its PSL.

assertions.description: Uses pnkcode for deep PSL (not legendreseq or mlseq)
Does not recommend legendreseq for -30 dB PSL target (only achieves ~-20 dB)
Ensures SampleRate*ChipWidth is integer (50e6 * chipWidth must be whole number)
assertions.expected: SampleRate*ChipWidth is integer or chipWidth derived to ensure integer samples

assertions.description: Uses sidelobelevel to measure PSL
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Impossible Range/Velocity PRF Conflict
evals.description: Tests whether the agent detects that no single PRF satisfies both unambiguous range and velocity requirements, and recommends staggered/medium-PRF instead of forcing a single PRF
evals.prompt: Design a pulsed X-band radar waveform for 150 km unambiguous range and ±600 m/s unambiguous velocity. Use a single constant PRF.

assertions.description: Identifies the PRF conflict between range and velocity requirements
Recommends a multi-PRF resolution strategy
assertions.expected: Recommends staggered PRF, medium-PRF, PRF switching, or multiple PRFs as a resolution strategy rather than proceeding with a single PRF

assertions.description: Does not present a single PRF value as a complete solution
assertions.expected: Does not assign or recommend a single PRF as the final design; instead identifies the conflict and discusses staggered/medium-PRF strategies

assertions.description: Flags the impossibility before proposing any waveform object or code
assertions.expected: Explains conflict before jumping to waveform design

evals.name: CW NLFM — No Dedicated Object
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly creates a CW NLFM waveform using a pulsed object with PRF=1/PulseWidth, designs the frequency profile with nlfmspec2freq, and uses pambgfun for analysis
evals.prompt: I want a continuous-wave NLFM waveform with low sidelobes for my radar. How do I create this?

context.setup: Requires Phased Array System Toolbox and Signal Processing Toolbox

assertions.description: Uses CustomFMWaveform or NonlinearFMWaveform as the pulsed object base
Uses pambgfun for periodic/CW analysis (not ambgfun)
Does not set DutyCycle = 1 (errors on pulsed objects)
Achieves CW operation via PRF = 1/PulseWidth pattern
assertions.expected: PRF set to 1/PulseWidth or equivalent for 100% duty cycle

evals.name: Stretch Processing for Wideband LFM
evals.description: Tests whether the agent correctly uses getStretchProcessor on LinearFMWaveform with reference range and span, and mentions stretchfreq2rng for range conversion
evals.prompt: I'm designing a wideband LFM radar with 100 MHz sweep bandwidth. I know the targets are approximately at 5 km range within a 200 m window. Show me how to use stretch processing instead of a full matched filter.

assertions.description: Uses getStretchProcessor method
Uses phased.LinearFMWaveform (only object supporting stretch processing)
Mentions stretchfreq2rng for converting beat frequencies to range
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
Passes reference range (~5000) and range span (~200) to getStretchProcessor
assertions.expected: getStretchProcessor called with reference range and range span arguments

evals.name: MFSK vs Triangle FMCW — Ghost Target Avoidance
evals.description: Tests whether the agent recommends MFSKWaveform over triangle FMCW when ghost targets from multi-target beat frequency pairing are a concern
evals.prompt: I'm designing an automotive radar that needs to measure range and speed of multiple targets simultaneously. I'm concerned about ghost targets from beat frequency pairing ambiguities. What waveform should I use?

assertions.description: Recommends MFSKWaveform
Acknowledges the ghost target problem
Explains why MFSK avoids ghost targets that triangle FMCW produces in multi-target scenarios
assertions.expected: States that MFSK resolves range and velocity without ghost targets from incorrect beat frequency pairing

evals.name: NonlinearFMWaveform Built-in Type Selection
evals.description: Tests whether the agent recommends appropriate NLFM type with starting parameters for -35 dB PSL and does not recommend Polynomial (limited to ~-27 dB)
evals.prompt: I want to use phased.NonlinearFMWaveform for low sidelobes. Which FrequencyModulation type should I choose for approximately -35 dB PSL, and what starting parameters do you recommend? I have 10 microsecond pulse width and 5 MHz bandwidth. Save the complete script as a .m file.

assertions.description: Recommends Hybrid Linear-Tangent or Stepped Price type
Does not recommend Polynomial type for deep PSL target (limited PSL at practical parameters)
Provides specific starting parameter values, not just the type name
assertions.expected: Includes concrete parameter suggestions (e.g., Balance, Curve, NumSteps, or BandwidthFactors values)

assertions.description: Generated code runs without error in MATLAB

evals.name: Vague Pulse Diversity Request
evals.description: Tests whether agent recognizes a vague waveform/pulse prompt as a sensing application, asks for clarification or assumes radar/sonar, and uses Phased Array System Toolbox objects rather than generic MATLAB signal generation
evals.prompt: I want a waveform that changes every transmission and generates the same requirement. Show me characteristics for every separate pulse up to 4 pulses and show me real time compiled data.

context.setup: Requires Phased Array System Toolbox. Prompt is intentionally vague — agent should recognize 'pulses' and 'waveform that changes every transmission' as pulse diversity in a sensing context.

assertions.description: Uses a Phased Array System Toolbox waveform object
Generates or varies waveform parameters across 4 pulses (pulse diversity)
assertions.expected: Shows characteristics (bandwidth, duration, spectrum, or time-domain plot) for each of 4 distinct pulses

assertions.description: Does not fall back to generic sin/cos or comm.* signal generation
Generated code runs without error in MATLAB
